questions.stem: Which chart type is best for showing how a single total is divided into parts, such as the share of a budget spent on each category?
questions.explanation: A pie chart shows parts of a whole, where each slice is a share of the total, which fits proportions of a budget well. A line chart is for trends over time, and a scatter chart is for relationships between two numeric variables, so neither suits a simple share breakdown.
questions.source: in the style of the Excel 2019 Bible

questions.stem: Which chart type is best for showing a trend in a value over a period of time, such as monthly sales across a year?
options: A bar chart of a single value
questions.explanation: A line chart plots points and joins them, making it easy to see how a value rises or falls over time, which is exactly what monthly sales need. A pie chart only shows a snapshot of shares and cannot display change across time.
questions.source: in the style of the Excel 2019 Bible

questions.stem: Which chart type is most suitable for comparing the totals of several separate categories, such as the population of five different cities?
questions.explanation: A column or bar chart shows one bar per category, so their heights or lengths can be compared at a glance, which suits comparing city populations. A pie chart works for shares of one whole and a line chart for trends, neither of which is comparing independent category totals.
questions.source: in the style of the Excel 2019 Bible

questions.stem: Which chart type is designed to show the relationship between two numeric variables, such as study hours against exam score for each student?
options: A single line with no x values
questions.explanation: A scatter chart plots paired numeric values as points, one axis per variable, which reveals whether two quantities move together. A pie chart shows shares of a whole and cannot display a relationship between two measured variables.
questions.source: in the style of the Excel 2019 Bible

questions.stem: In Excel, before you can insert a chart, what do you normally need to do first?
options: Save the file as a PDF
Select the data range you want to chart
questions.explanation: A chart is built from data, so you first select the cells (the labels and the values) that the chart should show, then choose a chart type from the Insert tab. Without selecting data there is nothing for Excel to plot.
questions.source: in the style of the Excel 2019 Bible

questions.stem: When you sort a column of numbers in ascending order, how are the values arranged?
questions.explanation: Ascending order runs from the smallest value up to the largest. For numbers that means low to high, and for text it means roughly A to Z. Descending order is the reverse, from largest down to smallest.
questions.source: in the style of the Excel 2019 Bible

questions.stem: When you sort a column of numbers in descending order, how are the values arranged?
questions.explanation: Descending order runs from the largest value down to the smallest, that is high to low for numbers. Ascending order is the opposite. Sorting rearranges the rows so the chosen column follows the order you pick.
questions.source: in the style of the Excel 2019 Bible

questions.stem: When sorting a list of names in a column from A to Z, what kind of sort is this?
questions.explanation: Arranging text from A to Z is an ascending alphabetical sort, the text equivalent of smallest to largest. Z to A would be descending. Sorting names this way makes a list easier to scan and search.
questions.source: in the style of the Excel 2019 Bible

questions.stem: You sort a table by the Marks column, but you want each student name to stay matched with its own mark. What must you do?
options: Include the whole table so all columns sort together with the rows
Select only the Marks column and sort just that
Sort the names separately from the marks
questions.explanation: To keep each row intact, you select the entire table (or let Excel expand the selection) so that when the Marks column is reordered, the matching names and other fields move with it. Sorting only one column would scramble the link between names and their marks.
questions.source: in the style of the Excel 2019 Bible

questions.stem: The small key on a chart that explains which colour or pattern represents each data series is called what?
questions.explanation: The legend maps each colour or marker to the series it represents, so a reader knows which line or bar is which. Gridlines are the background guide lines, an axis is a labelled side of the chart, and a data point is one plotted value.
questions.source: in the style of the Excel 2019 Bible

questions.stem: On a typical column chart the categories (such as the months) are listed along which axis?
questions.explanation: Category labels run along the horizontal axis at the bottom, while the measured values are read up the vertical axis. The legend names the series and the title describes the chart, but neither lists the categories.
questions.source: in the style of the Excel 2019 Bible

questions.stem: What is the main purpose of adding a clear chart title?
options: To change every cell to bold
To make the chart calculate faster
To tell the reader what the chart is showing
questions.explanation: A chart title states the subject of the chart so the audience understands what they are looking at without guessing. It does not affect calculation speed, sorting, or cell formatting, which are all separate matters.
questions.source: in the style of the Excel 2019 Bible

questions.stem: Which of the following is an example of simple cell formatting rather than a calculation?
options: Counting the numbers in a range
Finding the average of a column
Making the text in a cell bold
questions.explanation: Making text bold changes only the appearance of the cell, which is formatting. Adding, counting, and averaging all produce new computed values, so they are calculations. Formatting affects how data looks, not what it equals.
questions.source: in the style of the Excel 2019 Bible

questions.stem: You want the numbers in a column to display with a comma separating thousands and exactly two decimal places. Which Excel feature handles this?
questions.explanation: Number formatting controls how a value is displayed, including thousands separators and decimal places, without changing the stored number itself. Sorting reorders rows, charts visualise data, and spell check looks at text, none of which set a display format.
questions.source: in the style of the Excel 2019 Bible

questions.stem: A feature that automatically colours or highlights cells based on rules, for example shading every value below 50 in red, is called what?
questions.explanation: Conditional formatting applies formatting only when a cell meets a condition you set, so failing marks could turn red automatically. A pie chart is a visual, freeze panes locks rows or columns in view, and AutoSum inserts a SUM formula.
questions.source: in the style of the Excel 2019 Bible

questions.stem: Which Excel feature keeps the top header row visible on screen while you scroll down through a long list of data?
questions.explanation: Freeze Panes locks chosen rows or columns so they stay on screen as you scroll, which keeps the header row in view over a long table. Sorting reorders data, inserting a chart adds a graphic, and merging combines cells, none of which pin the header.
questions.source: in the style of the Excel 2019 Bible

questions.stem: If a chart is linked to its source cells and you change one of those values in the worksheet, what happens to the chart?
options: The worksheet value reverts to the old number
The chart stays frozen until you delete and rebuild it
The chart turns into plain text
The chart updates automatically to reflect the new value
questions.explanation: An Excel chart is tied to the cells it was built from, so editing those cells immediately redraws the chart with the new figures. You do not need to recreate the chart, which is one of the big advantages of charting live worksheet data.
questions.source: in the style of the Excel 2019 Bible

questions.stem: On a chart, the title written beside the vertical axis to say what the measured values represent (for example Sales in cedis) is called what?
questions.explanation: An axis title labels what an axis measures, such as the units on the vertical axis. A legend entry names a series, a gridline is a guide line in the background, and a data label shows the value of a specific point. Axis titles make the chart easier to interpret.
questions.source: in the style of the Excel 2019 Bible

questions.stem: Why is a pie chart usually a poor choice when you need to compare many categories whose values are all very close together?
options: Pie charts always sort the data for you
Pie charts cannot show more than two categories
Slices of similar size are hard to tell apart by eye
Pie charts only work with text, not numbers
questions.explanation: When slices are nearly equal, their angles look almost the same, so it is hard to judge which is bigger. A column or bar chart, where small differences in height are easier to read, is better for that case. Pie charts can hold more than two categories, but they shine only when the shares differ clearly.
questions.source: in the style of the Excel 2019 Bible

questions.stem: Small numbers or text placed directly on the bars or points of a chart to show each exact value are called what?
questions.explanation: Data labels print the actual value next to or on each bar, slice, or point, so readers can see exact figures rather than estimating from the axis. Axis titles label the axes, legends identify series, and sheet tabs switch worksheets.
questions.source: in the style of the Excel 2019 Bible

questions.stem: Which tab on the Excel Ribbon do you normally use to insert a chart into a worksheet?
questions.explanation: Charts are added from the Insert tab, which groups the chart types together. The Review tab holds tools like spell check and comments, the Formulas tab holds function tools, and the View tab controls how the sheet is displayed.
questions.source: in the style of the Excel 2019 Bible

questions.stem: A teacher has a table of student names and their total marks and wants to find the top five students quickly. Which action is the most direct way?
options: Sort the table by marks in descending order
Insert a pie chart of the names
questions.explanation: Sorting by marks from largest to smallest brings the highest scorers to the top, so the top five sit in the first five rows. A pie chart, bold formatting, and frozen headers do not reorder the data, so they would not surface the top students directly.
questions.source: in the style of the Excel 2019 Bible

questions.stem: What does sorting a range of data actually do to the rows?
options: It deletes any rows that contain text
It changes the numbers into a chart automatically
It rearranges the rows into a chosen order based on a column
It adds the values in every column
questions.explanation: Sorting reorders the existing rows according to the values in a chosen column, either ascending or descending, without removing or altering the data itself. It does not delete rows, total columns, or create a chart, which are all different operations.
questions.source: in the style of the Excel 2019 Bible

questions.stem: A stacked column chart shows several series piled on top of each other in one bar per category. What does the full height of each bar represent?
options: The total of all the series for that category
questions.explanation: In a stacked column the segments add up, so the overall height of a bar is the sum of all the series for that category, while each coloured segment is one series. This makes it easy to see both the parts and the combined total at once.
questions.source: in the style of the Excel 2019 Bible
